summary: Implemented deterministic finish-gate repair in task:lifecycle:settle. The settlement now repairs started tasks that already have grounded traces, reconstructs blocked missing-trace tasks, closes stale no-trace tasks as blocked/abandoned rather than successful completion, and preserves recent active work. Applied it to the current data vault: task lifecycle blockers dropped from 6 to 0, stale_active is 0, terminal_missing_trace is 0, and monitoring freshness was regenerated healthy at 11/11. App commit 63006db was pushed to origin/main. RAG remains the next unresolved workstream: current rag_eval still reports lexical_fallback 12/12 and hybrid 0/12.
decisions: Repair finish-gate blockers only when deterministic evidence exists or when the repair explicitly records blocked/abandoned instead of completion.
Started tasks with grounded terminal traces can safely inherit the trace outcome and trace_path.
Blocked tasks without trace can receive a reconstructed blocked trace from the task error/block reason.
Stale tasks without trace should be closed as blocked/abandoned with no-success wording, not silently marked completed.
Do not broad-push the data repo while review/session-derived dirty state remains broad.
next_steps: Start real RAG eval workstream: add explicit rag-golden.json and configure/prove dense-vector hybrid retrieval so rag_eval can pass with hybrid_contextual_v2.
Create scoped data-sync policy for already-applied review/finish settlement records without raw/session-local leakage.
Resolve the remaining 73 manual semantic review candidates by approve/reject/merge/hold workflow.
